articles.title: The foreign fighters who helped topple Assad — and why China worries about them
articles.summary: Thousands of Uyghurs became key fighters against Syria's Assad regime. For the first time, they agreed to be interviewed. NPR spent weeks with some of them to understand why they fled China for Syria.

articles.title: How a Jared Polis Came to Release Election Denier Tina Peters from Prison
articles.summary: Gov. Jared Polis’s decision to commute the sentence of Tina Peters came after months of tense discussions, including one with President Trump.

articles.title: Trump Administration Pushes Narrative of Christian Nation at Prayer Rally in D.C.
articles.summary: The nine-hour prayer event features speakers from President Trump’s cabinet and a program that connects the nation’s founding with Christianity.

articles.title: One clinic tracks the heavy toll Trump's immigration crackdown takes on mental health
articles.summary: Zocalo Health, a primary care organization, screens all its patients for depression, anxiety and suicidal thoughts. It documented a marked increase in those conditions since ICE enforcement actions be

articles.title: New burn bans and Trump's battle with immigration and DEI are impacting forest fires
articles.summary: Firefighters say setting fires on purpose is one of the best  ways to protect against massive wildfires later. But the Trump administration is banning or stalling preventative burning across the U.S.

articles.title: The Iran War Is Crippling One of the World’s Wealthiest Nations
articles.summary: Iranian attacks and the stoppage of seaborne transit have paralyzed Qatar’s vital gas exports, stalling the economic pivots intended to anchor the country’s growth.

articles.title: To Critics, Trump Remarks Reveal a Billionaire Out of Touch
articles.summary: The president has never pretended to be an ordinary American, but a recent “truth bomb” has opened him to criticism that he doesn’t grasp the economic strain of his war with Iran.

articles.title: Bus riders to Montgomery retrace old steps while fighting a new fight
articles.summary: Thousands of people rallied Saturday in Montgomery, Alabama, to push back against conservative states' efforts to dismantle congressional districts that helped secure Black political representation.

articles.title: Why Neutral Maps Could Empower Black Voters as Much as the Voting Rights Act
articles.summary: Our simulations debunk the notion that the act created artificial minority districts that wouldn’t otherwise exist.

articles.title: Ukraine conducts large-scale drone strikes on Russia, killing 4 and wounding 12 others
articles.summary: One of Ukraine's largest drone strikes on Russia killed at least four people, including three near Moscow, and wounded a dozen others, local authorities said Sunday.

articles.title: Inside the Secret History of the DeGrange Family
articles.summary: One fateful decision 100 years ago created parallel lives. How does a family broken by the bizarre rules of racism heal itself after three generations apart?

articles.title: In Iraqi Desert, Two Israeli Outposts Were Kept Secret for Months
articles.summary: Israel spent over a year preparing a covert site in Iraq for its operations against Iran, regional officials say. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.

articles.title: For Trump, Soaring Prices Test Voters’ Finances and Patience
articles.summary: Just months before another election that may hinge on the economy, the war in Iran has sent gas and other goods soaring.

articles.title: How a CEO and Trump donor is weaponizing tariffs against his rivals
articles.summary: Cambria CEO Marty Davis has successfully asked the U.S. government to put tariffs on quartz. His business competitors are crying foul.

articles.title: New York Magazine reviewing work of writer facing plagiarism allegations
articles.summary: The high-profile writer, who has written a forthcoming book about New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani, is being investigated by the magazine after being called out for copying the work of other writers.

articles.title: Louisiana senator who voted to convict Trump loses Republican primary
articles.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy is one of few remaining Republican senators who voted to convict President Trump after the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol. Trump endorsed his opponent, Rep. Julia Letlow.

articles.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
articles.summary: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

articles.title: Iranian man barely survives bombing, but wants war to go on
articles.summary: An Iranian man who was buried under rubble in US Israel bombing campaign says he wants the war to go on, and destroy the regime.

articles.title: Life on the edge: why Indonesians living near volcanoes won't leave
articles.summary: Indonesia has more than 120 active volcanoes. On the island of Java, thousands live alongside Mount Merapi, constantly balancing risks -- and most say they won't leave.

articles.title: Timmy the humpback whale found dead off Danish coast
articles.summary: A humpback whale found dead this week off a Danish island has been identified as the animal released two weeks ago in a spectacular and controversial rescue effort after repeatedly becoming stranded o

articles.title: The war in Ukraine is at a standstill as societal fatigue grows in Russia
articles.summary: With the peace process stalled, there are signs of growing public fatigue in Russia with the Kremlin's war as Ukraine uses robotic warfare to stay in the fight.

articles.title: The townspeople of Vilseck, Germany, worry that Trump may pull out 5,000 U.S. troops
articles.summary: President Trump's troop withdrawal threat rattles residents of a small Bavarian town reliant on U.S. military personnel and their families for both income and friendships built over decades.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
